description: Outcome-level upstream-vs-Ultra deep problem-solving behavior cases. These cases focus on whether the runtime can ground, execute, verify, synthesize, recover, and keep momentum on hard tasks.

cases.upstream_behavior: Hard tasks are reframed into an evidence-first loop with tool use, memory retrieval, output-shape discipline, and self-checking before final response.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra injects an internal runtime reformulation before the latest user message that requires anti-scheming evidence discipline, ContextLattice-first grounding, concrete action, iterative objective momentum, and contradiction self-audit.
cases.why: This directly governs deep problem solving before any model response is produced, rather than relying on general personality text.

cases.upstream_behavior: For long-running objectives, the agent should continue working instead of returning plan-only or status-only responses.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra detects status-heavy, defer-only, or tool-less objective turns and injects a continuation pass requiring concrete action, verifiable evidence, measurable delta, and next action.
cases.why: This closes a deep-behavior failure mode where an agent sounds thoughtful but stops before doing work.

cases.upstream_behavior: Relevant durable memory should be retrieved before solving, and objective context should guide later actions.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra prefers ContextLattice as the memory backbone, sends project/topic search payloads, pressure-tunes retrieval mode, and autopins active objective topics.
cases.why: This makes the memory backbone explicit and testable for hard tasks that depend on prior context.

cases.upstream_behavior: Repository research should inspect local evidence, avoid repetitive low-signal loops, and only finalize with concrete workstream evidence or an explicit blocker.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra applies repo-review tool profiles, trims repeated discovery loops, detects path-scoped research intent, and retries finalization when repo plans lack explicit evidence.
cases.why: This is the core behavior the user cares about in codebase problem solving: local evidence before conclusion.

cases.upstream_behavior: Web research should use web tools when requested and synthesize from source-backed evidence rather than unsupported claims.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra requires web-tool evidence, URLs, source-quality counts, and compact source rendering before finalizing web-research answers.
cases.why: This targets the exact deep-research behavior that makes upstream strong: source-backed synthesis instead of generic answer generation.

cases.upstream_behavior: Difficult questions should surface disagreement, counter-cases, confidence, and thin-evidence limitations instead of collapsing to a single unsupported answer.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra has a quorum fanout path with independent voters, explicit counter-argument requirements, degraded-output detection, synthesis rules, and persisted quorum artifacts.
cases.why: This is an explicit deep-synthesis mechanism beyond baseline single-model turn handling.

cases.upstream_behavior: Tool-heavy problem solving should recover from malformed tool payloads, repeated tool failures, and stream interruptions without pretending success.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra detects provider tool-payload schema rejection, trips loop guards on consecutive full-failure turns, preserves partial successes, and retries recoverable stream/tool-call drops.
cases.why: Deep problem solving depends on robust tool iteration, not only initial tool availability.

cases.upstream_behavior: The agent should route simple and complex turns appropriately, learn from route outcomes, and preserve provider/auth recovery behavior.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra includes smart model routing, route learning persistence/decay, provider fallback chains, transient retry classification, and OAuth/provider auth repair tests.
cases.why: Deep behavior quality depends on choosing the right model path and recovering when a provider path degrades.

cases.upstream_behavior: Before final answers, the agent should reject placeholders, fake artifacts, duplicate filler, unsupported research claims, and narration of actions that were not executed.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra retries finalization for missing evidence paths, placeholder text, fake attachments, duplicate lines, and intent-narration without evidence.
cases.why: This is the last line of defense between internal reasoning and user-visible deep-work output.

cases.upstream_behavior: The agent preserves useful context while preventing context bloat, prompt-injection drift, and memory leakage into the final user-visible response.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra loads workspace context, scans for injection, preserves system messages during compression, and scrubs memory context blocks from streamed callbacks/final messages.
cases.why: This protects the substrate needed for multi-step problem solving.
